Sheila Robello, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Peter Wilms, Executor, in court.

David Sauers, via Zoom, for Michelle Keller, Beneficiary.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Sep-20-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-29-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello, Esq., counsel for Peter Wilms, Executor; David Sauers, Esq., counsel for Michele Keller, Beneficiary and Objector (all appearances by video conference)

At the outset, Mr. Sauers advises that while his client continues to object to the way the rent for the various real properties was handled, he will reserve those objections for later as he and Ms. Robello are in meaningful discussions regarding the same.

Ms. Robello concurs and reports that the Second and Final Account and Petition for Final Distribution can be filed within 60 days to allow time for more discussions with Mr. Sauers. She reports that the Crestline property has been sold.

The Court queries Ms. Robello as to an oddly identified disbursement of $3,409. Ms. Robello advises that the original designation was in error and that the funds where actually for the insurance premium for the Mission property. Mr. Sauers notes that most of his objections to the First Account have been resolved and that the accounting can now be allowed. As such, the Court takes the accounting petition under submission with the intent to grant the same.

Mr. Sauers also advises the Court that his earlier objections to the appraised value of the Mission property have also been resolved and he will consent to the award of attorney fees of $50,000. The Court notes the request is for $54,000 and takes the matter under submission. Additionally, Mr. Sauers will withdraw his objections to the proposed preliminary distribution. The Court takes the preliminary distribution petition under submission with the intent to grant the same.

After further discussion, the Court sets hearing on the status of the Second Accounting - and hopefully the Petition for Final Distribution - for September 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Accounting/ Petition for Final Distribution must be filed on or before September 6, 2023.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Choi. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-06-2023 Continued To Jun-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-25-2023 Continued To Jun-06-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Peter Wilms (executor).
David Sauers appeared by zoom for Michelle Keller (beneficiary/objector).

Mrs. Robello stated that the statements were just uploaded, that she has not served the supplement, but the documents were sent to Mr. Sauers yesterday and that she plans on responding to the objections. Mr. Sauers stated that the executor is living on the property rent free and also using the Truckee property as a vacation property without any form of payment, and that there are checks worth $70,000.00 that are unidentified. The Amended First Account and Report of Executor, etc. is continued to June 6, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mrs. Robello to respond and clear up all of the issues that were raised in the Probate Examiner's notes. All pleadings are to be filed one week before the hearing/by May 30, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Sheila Robello appearing via zoom for executor Peter Wilms.
David Savers appearing via zoom for beneficiary Michelle Keller.

Mrs. Robelo stated that they had some settlement discussion, requested three weeks to resolve any remaining questions, and stated that they are actively discussing settlement. Mr. Sauers is amenable to a continuance. Mrs. Robello stated that she filed a response to examiners notes yesterday and that she filed a comprehensive response to the objections. The amended first account and report of executor is continued to April 25, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any additional responses to the notes and to the objections are to be filed by April 18, 2023.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Martinez. Not reported.

The court queries counsel regarding how the court can assist the parties in resolving this matter. Ms. Robello reports that she and Mr. Sauers have begun preliminary discussions and asks for a continuance to allow time for additional settlement discussions. ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of First Two-Year Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Dec-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for and with Peter Wilms, Executor.

The court acknowledges the one-year accounting filed by counsel and points to the delay of a two-year accounting. Ms. Robello reports of still working on balancing the two-year accounting and of filing the one-year accounting in the interim. The court voices its concern with the delay in filing the two-year accounting. Ms. Robello reports of the intent to file an amended petition to include the two-year accounting. The court advises that it intends to grant counsel's request for a continuance but warns counsel that the court may consider suspending the Executor if the required account is not filed by the next calling. The court notes that the bookkeeper is not present today as was ordered by the court at the previous calling. The court reminds counsel that best efforts should be made to comply with court orders.

After additional discussion, the Status hearing is continued to Dec-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of First Two-Year Accounting

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for Peter Wilms, Personal Representative.

The court queries Ms. Robello regarding the status of the accounting in this matter. Ms. Robello reports that the bookkeeper is working on the schedules and expects the accounting to be completed and filed within thirty days and requests a continuance accordingly. Ms. Robello is reminded that her report at the last calling of this matter was similar to today's. The court advises Ms. Robello that if the accounting is not filed by the next calling of this matter, the court orders her client and the bookkeeper to appear to explain the delay.

The Status Hearing is continued to Nov-02-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. William Barry. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for Peter Wilms, Executor; David Sauers, in court, for Michelle Keller, Beneficiary.

Ms. Robello reports on the current status of this matter and of the real properties listed for sale and that the parties wish to sell and distribute proceeds to avoid co-ownership. Mr. Sauers reports that he has no objection to the status petition but advises that he would like an accounting. The court reminds the parties that the court does not want to wait until all properties are sold in order to completely resolve this matter and expects the parties to meet and confer in order to reach a resolution as soon as possible. The court advises Ms. Robello that it is ordering a two-year accounting from date of death, two years forward, which is June of 2019 to June of 2021.

After additional discussion, the Status Petition is taken under submission. A Status of First Two-Year Accounting is set for Jun-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Ms. Robello is instructed to file the Accounting by Jun-03-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Glisson. Not reported.
